teamObjective.explanation: Leading the 3♣ is the safest opening lead: it expends the weakest card, keeps flexibility, and does not hand the near-finishing opponent a higher card to exploit.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) holds 3 cards; their exact composition is unknown. With no public plays yet, they likely have a mix of ranks/suits and may be able to cover a low lead or signal strength later.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 has only 2 cards and is near finishing, suggesting they may hold a high card or trump to exit soon. Opponent player 3 has 3 cards and is also unknown. Opponents will try to win this trick cheaply or let partner waste cards.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3♣ is the safest opening lead: it expends the weakest card, keeps flexibility, and does not hand the near-finishing opponent a higher card to exploit.
